back_translation: The word of Samuel came to all Israel. Israel went out to meet the Philistines for war, and they camped by Ebenezer, and the Philistines camped at Aphek.
The Philistines arrayed to meet Israel, and the battle spread, and Israel was defeated before the Philistines, and they struck in the army in the field about four thousand men.

The people came to the camp, and the elders of Israel said, 'Why has the LORD struck us today before the Philistines? Let us bring to ourselves from Shiloh the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and let it come into our midst and save us from the hand of our enemies.'

The people sent to Shiloh, and they brought up from there the ark of the covenant of the LORD of Hosts who sits of the cherubim, and there were the two sons of Eli with the ark of the covenant of God, Hophni and Phinehas.
It was as the ark of the covenant of the LORD came into the camp, all Israel shouted a great shout, and the earth shook.
The Philistines heard the sound of the shouting and said, 'What is this sound of the great shouting in the camp of the Hebrews?' And they knew that the ark of the LORD had come to the camp.
The Philistines feared, for they said, 'Gods have come to the camp.' And they said, 'Woe to us, for it has not been like this yesterday or before.'
'Woe to us! Who will deliver us from the hand of these mighty gods? These are the gods who struck Egypt with every plague in the wilderness.'
'Strengthen yourselves and be men, O Philistines, lest you serve the Hebrews as they served you. Be men and fight.'
The Philistines fought, and Israel was defeated, and they fled each man to his tent, and the slaughter was very great — and there fell from Israel thirty thousand foot soldiers.
The ark of God was taken, and the two sons of Eli died — Hophni and Phinehas.
A Benjamite man ran from the battle line and came to Shiloh on that day, and his clothes were torn and earth was on his head.

He came, and behold, Eli was sitting on the chair at the hand of the road, watching, because his heart was trembling over the ark of God; and the man came to report in the city, and all the city cried out.
Eli heard the sound of the crying out and said, 'What is this sound of commotion?' And the man hurried and came and told Eli.
And Eli was a son of ninety-eight years, and his eyes were set, and he was not able to see.
The man said to Eli, 'I have come from the battle line, and I from the battle line fled today.' He said, 'What was the thing that happened, my son?'

The messenger answered and said, 'Israel fled before the Philistines, and also a great slaughter was in the people, and also your two sons died — Hophni and Phinehas — and the ark of God has been taken.'

It was as he mentioned the ark of God, that he fell from upon the chair backward by the hand of the gate, and his neck was broken, and he died, because the man was old and heavy. He had judged Israel forty years.

His daughter-in-law, the wife of Phinehas, was pregnant about to give birth, and she heard the report concerning the taking of the ark of God, and the death of her father-in-law and her husband, and she bowed down and gave birth, because her pains had turned upon her.
At the time of her dying, those standing over her said, 'Do not fear, for you have given birth to a son.' But she did not answer and did not set her heart.
She called the boy Ichabod, saying, 'Glory has departed from Israel,' concerning the taking of the ark of God, and concerning her father-in-law and her husband.
She said, 'Glory has departed from Israel, for the ark of God has been taken.'
